passages.title: Maple Syrup Season in Ontario
passages.body:
Each spring, when nights are still cold but daytime temperatures climb above freezing, maple sap begins to flow in sugar bushes across Ontario and Quebec. Producers tap maple trees by drilling small holes and hanging buckets or tubes that collect the clear, watery sap. It takes about 40 litres of sap — sometimes more — to make just one litre of syrup, because sap is mostly water.

The collected sap is boiled for many hours in a sugar shack until enough water evaporates and the liquid thickens into golden syrup. The colour and flavour can vary depending on how early or late in the season the sap was gathered. Many farms welcome visitors during "sugar bush" weekends, offering tours, samples, and pancakes — a tradition that connects today's families to practices that go back generations.

questions.prompt: According to the passage, when does maple sap usually begin to flow?
choices: Only in the middle of summer
When nights are cold but daytime temperatures rise above freezing
Whenever it rains for a week
After all the leaves have fallen
questions.answer: When nights are cold but daytime temperatures rise above freezing

questions.prompt: About how much sap does the passage say is needed to make one litre of syrup?
questions.answer: About 40 litres or more

questions.prompt: Why must producers boil the sap for a long time?
choices: To add sugar from the air
To evaporate water so the liquid thickens into syrup
questions.answer: To evaporate water so the liquid thickens into syrup

questions.prompt: What can cause maple syrup to differ in colour and flavour?
choices: Whether the sap came from oak trees
How early or late in the season the sap was gathered
The size of the pancakes served
Whether visitors came on a weekend
questions.answer: How early or late in the season the sap was gathered

questions.prompt: The author mentions pancakes and tours mainly to show that —
choices: maple farms only sell pancakes, not syrup
maple syrup production is a tradition that families still share today
questions.answer: maple syrup production is a tradition that families still share today

questions.prompt: Read: "The experiment failed because the team rushed the final steps." Which word is closest in meaning to "rushed" here?
questions.answer: hurried

questions.prompt: Maya scores 88, 92, and 84 on three quizzes. Her friend says, "You always get above 95." Why is the friend's statement weak?
choices: Because Maya did not take any quizzes
Because the scores shown are all below 95
Because 88 is the highest possible score
Because averages cannot be calculated with three numbers
questions.answer: Because the scores shown are all below 95

questions.prompt: Which is the best closing sentence for a paragraph that explains how to plant a seed?
choices: Therefore, water the soil gently and place the pot where it gets suitable light.
I like pizza more than gardening.
Once upon a time there was a dragon.
questions.answer: Therefore, water the soil gently and place the pot where it gets suitable light.

questions.prompt: "The evidence is scant." In this sentence, "scant" most likely means —
questions.answer: very little
questions.hintContext: If evidence is scant, do you have a lot to work with or almost none?

questions.prompt: A poster claims: "Our juice has vitamins, so drinking it cures every illness." What logical problem does this have?
choices: It leaps from one true idea to an extreme conclusion that is not supported
questions.answer: It leaps from one true idea to an extreme conclusion that is not supported

questions.prompt: Which pair are synonyms?
questions.answer: rapid / swift

questions.prompt: The mayor announced a new skate park. A critic writes, "This is a frivolous waste when roads need repair." The critic's tone is mainly —
questions.answer: disapproving

questions.prompt: If all robins are birds, and this animal is not a bird, which conclusion is valid?
questions.answer: It cannot be a robin
questions.hintContext: Robins are a type of bird. If something is not a bird, can it still be a robin?

questions.prompt: Which revision fixes the run-on? "We hiked to the ridge the view was incredible."
choices: We hiked to the ridge the view, was incredible.
We hiked to the ridge, and the view was incredible.
We hiked to the ridge the view were incredible.
We hiked, to the ridge the view was incredible.
questions.answer: We hiked to the ridge, and the view was incredible.

questions.prompt: A student concludes: "The book is boring because I only read the first page." This is weak because —
choices: one page may not represent the whole book
questions.answer: one page may not represent the whole book

questions.prompt: "She delivered the speech with poise." "Poise" suggests she was —
questions.answer: calm and self-controlled

questions.prompt: Which question is best for researching "how compost helps gardens"?
choices: How does compost improve soil for plants?
Why are dogs better than cats?
questions.answer: How does compost improve soil for plants?

questions.prompt: Read: "Unlike his brother, Leo rarely raises his voice." What does this imply about Leo?
choices: Leo is usually quiet or calm when speaking
questions.answer: Leo is usually quiet or calm when speaking

questions.prompt: A survey asked only people leaving a hockey game, "Is hockey the best sport?" Why might results be biased?
choices: Because the sample probably includes many hockey fans already
Because hockey is not a sport
Because Grade 5 students cannot answer surveys
questions.answer: Because the sample probably includes many hockey fans already

questions.prompt: Which transition best shows contrast? "Several species adapted to the cold. ____, others migrated south."
questions.answer: However
